parentQuestion: White matter is found in all of the following locations EXCEPT the ___.

scaffolds.q: What is the difference between gray matter and white matter?
options: Gray matter has cell bodies; white matter has myelinated axons
Gray matter is in the skull; white matter is outside
White matter has cell bodies; gray matter has axons
scaffolds.explain: GRAY MATTER contains neuron CELL BODIES, dendrites, and synapses (computation). WHITE MATTER contains MYELINATED AXON bundles (transmission). Different functions, different tissue types.

wrongExplains.0: They are distinct tissue types with different compositions and functions.
wrongExplains.2: Both are inside the skull as brain tissue. Their location within the brain differs.
wrongExplains.3: That's reversed. Gray matter has cell bodies; white matter has axons.

scaffolds.q: Where is gray matter located in the BRAIN?
options: On the outside (cortex), covering white matter
scaffolds.explain: In the BRAIN, gray matter is on the OUTSIDE (cortex) and white matter is DEEPER INSIDE. The brain puts its processing layer on the surface, with transmission cables running beneath.

wrongExplains.0: In the brain, gray matter (cortex) is on the surface. Deep inside is white matter (and basal nuclei).
wrongExplains.2: Gray matter is also in the cerebral cortex, not just the brainstem.
wrongExplains.3: All brain tissue is inside the skull.

scaffolds.q: Where is gray matter located in the SPINAL CORD?
options: On the outside, forming a shell
On the inside, forming a butterfly shape
There is no gray matter in the spinal cord
scaffolds.explain: In the SPINAL CORD, the arrangement is FLIPPED. Gray matter forms the INNER BUTTERFLY shape; WHITE MATTER forms the OUTER shell with ascending/descending tracts.

wrongExplains.0: The outer shell is white matter. Gray matter is the inner butterfly.
wrongExplains.2: Gray matter extends throughout the spinal cord length.
wrongExplains.3: The spinal cord definitely has gray matter — it's the central butterfly shape.

scaffolds.q: What type of tissue is the corpus callosum?
options: White matter (massive bundle of myelinated axons)
scaffolds.explain: The CORPUS CALLOSUM is WHITE MATTER — the brain's largest commissure with about 200 million myelinated axons connecting the hemispheres.

wrongExplains.0: The corpus callosum is white matter (axon bundles), not gray matter (cell bodies).
wrongExplains.2: The corpus callosum is neural tissue, not bone.
wrongExplains.3: It's solid white matter, not fluid.

scaffolds.q: What type of tissue are the corticospinal tracts?
scaffolds.explain: The CORTICOSPINAL TRACTS are WHITE MATTER — projection fibers carrying motor commands from motor cortex down to spinal motor neurons. They're myelinated axon bundles.

wrongExplains.0: Tracts are white matter (axon bundles). Gray matter has cell bodies.
wrongExplains.2: Neural tracts are soft tissue, not bone.
wrongExplains.3: Neural tracts are in the nervous system, not muscle tissue.

scaffolds.q: What type of tissue is in the outer portion of the spinal cord?
scaffolds.explain: The OUTER SPINAL CORD contains WHITE MATTER — dorsal columns (sensory), lateral columns (motor and sensory), and ventral columns. The ascending and descending tracts are here.

wrongExplains.0: Gray matter is in the center (butterfly). White matter forms the outer shell.
wrongExplains.2: The spinal cord is neural tissue, protected by vertebral bone but not made of bone.
wrongExplains.3: Both motor and sensory tracts are in the outer white matter.

scaffolds.q: What type of tissue is the cerebral cortex?
scaffolds.explain: The CEREBRAL CORTEX is GRAY MATTER — packed with neuron cell bodies, dendrites, and synapses. This is where conscious processing happens, NOT transmission.

wrongExplains.0: The cortex is gray matter. White matter is beneath the cortex.
wrongExplains.2: The cortex is neural tissue, not bone.
wrongExplains.3: The cortex is solid gray matter, not fluid.

options: Outer spinal cord, corpus callosum, corticospinal tracts
scaffolds.explain: WHITE MATTER is found in the OUTER SPINAL CORD (tracts), CORPUS CALLOSUM (commissure), and CORTICOSPINAL TRACTS (projection fibers). The cerebral cortex is the exception — it's gray matter.

wrongExplains.0: White matter is also in the brain — corpus callosum, internal capsule, corona radiata, etc.
wrongExplains.2: The cerebral cortex is gray matter, not white matter.
wrongExplains.3: The skull is bone. White matter is neural tissue inside the brain and spinal cord.

scaffolds.q: White matter is found in all locations EXCEPT the ___.
options: Outer portion of the spinal cord
scaffolds.explain: The CEREBRAL CORTEX is the exception — it's GRAY MATTER (processing tissue). The other options (outer spinal cord, corpus callosum, corticospinal tracts) are all WHITE MATTER structures.

wrongExplains.0: The outer spinal cord is white matter — it contains ascending and descending tracts.
wrongExplains.1: The corpus callosum is white matter — it's the largest commissure with 200 million axons.
wrongExplains.3: The corticospinal tracts are white matter — motor projection fibers.
